result.title: Can AI help Imperial College of London and the NHS improve the mammography screening process?
result.description:
Google partnered with Imperial College London and three NHS trusts on the Artificial Intelligence in Mammography Screening (AIMS) study to explore how an AI reader, integrated in place of a second human reader in the UK’s existing two reader plus arbitration system, performs in a real-world setting.

This is an investigational AI tool not yet approved for clinical use; it is not intended to replace professional medical judgment. All imagery has been de-identified per data protection legislation.
result.transcript_error: Transcript requests are blocked from the current IP.

result.title: Helping Radiologists Detect Breast Cancer with AI
result.description:
Breast cancer affects 1 in 8 women worldwide, and in the UK, a shortage of radiologists puts immense pressure on staff in radiology departments to deal with the backlog of people waiting for scans. To help reduce the burden on resources and staff, new ways of screening need to be investigated. 

Google partnered with Imperial College London and three NHS trusts on the Artificial Intelligence in Mammography Screening (AIMS) study to explore how an AI reader, integrated in place of a second human reader in the UK’s existing two reader plus arbitration system, performs in a real-world setting.

Results of the AIMS study, recently published in “Nature Cancer,” showed that an AI reader could successfully integrate in to the UK’s existing system in place of a human reader, reducing the overall workload of radiologists and allowing them to focus their time and care on patients who needed it the most. 

Note: The patients featured in this video did not participate in the study and are sharing their personal experiences with the mammography process. They were compensated for their time.

To learn more about the AIMS study, visit https://www.nature.com/articles/s43018-026-01128-z   

To learn more about how AI can improve breast cancer detection in the UK, check out The Keyword: https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/health/google-ai-breast-cancer-detection/

result.title: Roots. Mentors. Legacy. Innovation -- shaping the limitless possibilities of Dr. Marian Croak
result.description: The Art of Possible is a documentary tracing the legacy of Dr. Marian Croak, a pioneer whose career was shaped by family, colleagues, mentors, and collaborators. These formative relationships and moments drove her innovation forward, establishing Dr. Croak as the crucial link between foundational technology and the future of responsible AI. She pioneered Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP)—the technology powering every internet call today—holds over 200 patents, and invented critical applications like text-to-donate technology. Now, asvVP Human Centered AI and Foundational ML, she continues to ensure that advanced research is built on ethical foundations for the benefit of humanity. Join us as we celebrate Dr. Croak.

result.title: Happy International Women’s Day #GoogleDoodle
result.description: This International Women’s Day, the #GoogleDoodle spotlights the breakthroughs from women in STEM that helped shape the world we live in today. From the skies to the seas to lifesaving medical discoveries, these pioneers proved that curiosity knows no bounds.
